title: Curmudgeon rubric amendment — separate truth-preservation from design-standards-compliance
summary: Curmudgeon applied its dome-content adversarial register to the Phase A design-refresh EXPs and produced six pending-human flags, of which only one was a real blocker and one required a post-integration visual check. The other four were design-standards pedantry — proposal-prose inconsistency, internal JND thresholds, theoretical fallback-CSS concerns, test-regex robustness. Curmudgeon needs a rubric amendment that distinguishes the two review registers.
principle: Curmudgeons adversarial lens is calibrated for truth-preservation. For design/a11y/typography/token/layout EXPs, the strict register should remain on: design choices that undermine, obscure, distort, or misrepresent a truth claim on the rendered page (e.g., color semantics that flatten a severity hierarchy, typography that buries a headline claim, contrast that renders a refutation unreadable, spacing that collapses a semantic distinction). The register should RELAX on: proposal-prose inconsistency, internal EXP estimates of cost/bandwidth/deltaE, theoretical fallback states that never fire in production, test-harness robustness concerns without current-DOM failure, style-guide adherence without truth impact.

context.triggering_incident: Phase A design refresh (EXP-202, 207-213) produced six pending-human ISS from curmudgeon review: ISS-1334, 1346, 1365, 1366, 1367, 1368. Operator triaged via HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-ISS-DISPOSITIONS-001.

dispositions_as_calibration_data.why: Line 250 is the Silent-failures scorecard border; leaving one color unmigrated while others migrate could flatten the semantic severity hierarchy (Silent tier must read harsher than Acknowledged tier). This is design obscuring truth.

dispositions_as_calibration_data.iss: ISS-1368 (EXP-210 light-theme deltaE only discloses dark-theme in proposal prose)
dispositions_as_calibration_data.why: The proposal-prose disclosure gap is pedantry. But the underlying question — does the new light-theme refuted color still read as the harsher-tier color — is a truth-preservation check that should happen on the rendered page, not against the proposal prose.

dispositions_as_calibration_data.iss: ISS-1365 (EXP-208 graceful-degradation fallback refs undefined CSS vars)
dispositions_as_calibration_data.why: Fallback state never fires in production (EXP-202 hard prerequisite). No on-page truth impact because the render-path is unreachable.
Test harness concern; test passes on current DOM. No truth impact.

dispositions_as_calibration_data.iss: ISS-1346 (EXP-202 bandwidth estimate undershoot in proposal prose)
dispositions_as_calibration_data.why: Estimate lives in proposal prose, not on rendered page. No truth impact.

dispositions_as_calibration_data.iss: ISS-1334 (EXP-202 privacy false-premise re Google Fonts)
dispositions_as_calibration_data.why: Resolved by operator clarifications before this rubric triage.

context.yield_ratio: 6 flags → 1 true blocker (1367) + 1 post-integration check (1368) + 4 false-positives by truth-impact standard. A 4:1 false-positive ratio on a design EXP batch burns operator triage time and erodes trust in the signal.

proposed_fix_spec.deliverable: A PROP proposing a curmudgeon.md amendment that introduces a truth-vs-standards discriminator for design-category reviews.
proposed_fix_spec.direction_of_travel: Not a relaxation of curmudgeons adversarial stance. A re-targeting of it. Curmudgeon should remain strict — arguably MORE strict — on the narrow set of design choices that can distort truth on the rendered page, and should consciously de-prioritize pedantry that does not.

keep_adversarial_on: Color semantics that flatten, invert, or erase a severity or status hierarchy on the rendered page
Typography choices that bury or demote a truth-bearing claim (headline, refutation, killshot, methodology)
Contrast failures that make claim text unreadable or near-unreadable
Spacing or layout that collapses a semantic distinction (e.g., acknowledged vs. silent failures)
Any a11y-gated concern with functional impact (WCAG contrast below AA, keyboard-nav loss, screen-reader context loss)
Any design choice that alters how a reader would parse a reviewed claim
relax_on: Proposal-prose inconsistency (e.g., graceful-degradation claims about states that never fire)
Internal EXP estimates of cost/bandwidth/deltaE/JND — provided the estimate does not anchor an integration decision that affects on-page truth
Theoretical fallback-CSS correctness when the fallback render path is unreachable in production
Test-harness robustness concerns without current-DOM failure
Style-guide adherence that does not affect on-page truth parsing
Proposal-prose disclosure gaps about deltas that are better evaluated against the rendered page post-integration

thinking_prompts: How does curmudgeon decide which rubric applies? EXP category field? Tag-based? First-pass heuristic?
Does this need a new review section in curmudgeon.md or amendment to existing Mode 1/0d/0c?
How should findings under the relaxed rubric be surfaced? Still as ISS? As advisories-only? Inline in the review JSON with a reduced severity class?
Should a11y-functional concerns get their own callout so the relaxation does not accidentally swallow them? Probably yes.

How to avoid swinging too far: if curmudgeon relaxes on all design prose, the system could miss a genuine truth-obscuring design choice that happens to be framed in proposal prose. What is the safety net?
Retroactive calibration: run the proposed rubric against the 6 Phase A ISS. Does it land on the same dispositions the operator did? If not, why, and is the rubric or the operator wrong?

deliverable_requested.format: PROP file in monitor/tinker/proposals/ following existing PROP-* naming conventions.
contents: Retroactive calibration: run the proposed rubric against the 6 Phase A ISS (1334, 1346, 1365, 1366, 1367, 1368). Report rubric outcome per ISS and compare to operator dispositions. Explain any divergence.
Proposed amendment to curmudgeon.md — exact text changes, not a description. Probably a new sub-section or a decision-tree at the top of Mode 1 that branches on EXP category.
Proposed EXP category or tag mechanism so curmudgeon can route reliably. Analyst-side prompt change may be needed to stamp category onto new EXPs.
Migration plan: do we apply the amendment immediately or shadow-run? Recommend one.
Rollback plan: if the rubric suppresses a finding that later turns out to be truth-impacting, how do we notice and revert?
Explicit a11y carveout: WCAG functional concerns must not be swallowed by the relaxation.
Proposed severity-class labeling: under the new rubric, what is the right output grade for relaxed-register findings? Advisory? Info? A reduced severity on the existing scale?

non_goals: Do NOT propose removing curmudgeons adversarial stance entirely. The current register is correct for dome-content work and must remain unchanged there.
Do NOT propose that curmudgeon skip design EXPs entirely. Relaxation of register is not the same as skipping review.
Do NOT blur the a11y carveout. Functional accessibility concerns are truth-impacting (a reader who cannot read the claim cannot evaluate it).

related_prompt_files: monitor/prompts/decider.md (for triage routing if severity-class changes)

completion_criteria: Tinker produces a PROP file addressing the deliverable list. Operator reviews + approves the retroactive-calibration result and the exact prompt patch before the curmudgeon.md change is applied through the normal clone-side edit → build.js publish flow.
